questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (a), all follow the same logic as follows: TQWG T - 3 = Q T + 3 = W Reverse alphabet of T = G JGMQ J - 3 = G J + 3 = M Reverse alphabet of J = Q WTZD W - 3 = T W + 3 = Z Reverse alphabet of W = D

questions.question: The series is given, with one term missing. Choose amongst the given responses and complete the series. ACBD, FHGI, KMLN, __.
questions.explanation: 2nd letter of each term is at the gap of 1 alphabet next to 1st letter. 3rd letter is the previous alphabet of 2nd letter and 4th letter is at the gap of 1 alphabet next to 3rd letter. Pattern is illustrated by following figure; ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3637/image002.jpg) So, next term is; PRQS.

questions.question: Five persons Kapil, Khushi, Kamal, Koyal and Karan had different annual incomes. Khushi earned more than only one other person. Koyal earned more than Kamal but less than Karan. Kapil earned less than Koyal but did not earn the least. Who among the following earned the 3rd lowest?
questions.explanation: Khushi earned more than only one other person. __ > __ > __ > Khushi > __ Koyal earned more than Kamal but less than Karan. Karan> Koyal> Kamal Kapil earned less than Koyal but did not earn the least. Koyal > Kapil Karan> Koyal> Kapil/Kamal Karan earns more than atleast 3 persons, thus we have: Karan > Koyal > Kapil > Khushi > Kamal Thus, Kapil earned the 3rd lowest

questions.question: Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of figure after folding and cutting. Problem Figure ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8386/image022.png) Solution Figure ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8386/image023.png)
questions.explanation: After first unfold, there would be four squares; one each at the top and the bottom on the left side and two at the center in the right side. After final unfold, there would be 8 squares forming 'X' shape.

questions.question: The following figure shows different positions of a dice. What number will come opposite to 5? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/63934/image015.png)
questions.explanation: From the 1st and 3rd figure, the number 6 is on the face opposite to number 1 From 2nd and 3rd figure, the number 4 is on the face opposite to number 2 Hence, the number 3 is on the face opposite to number 5

questions.question: In a certain code, POSTER is written as OP1SF9, COWARD is written as BP5ZS4. How is RIBBON written in that code?
questions.explanation: The first letter is reduced by 1, the second increased by 1. For the third letter, the code is the digit sum of the position of that letter. e.g. S is 19th letter, 1+9=10, 1+0=1. Similarly, R is 18th letter, 1+8=9; W is 23, 2+3=5; D is 4. Hence RIBBON will be coded as QJ2AP5

questions.question: Which one set of letters/numbers when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? 1 _ 3 1 1 _ 2 8 _ 1 9 7 2 _ 4 _
questions.explanation: The series is, 113, 123, 133, 143

questions.question: If A=B>C≥D=E<F≤G, which of the following expressions is definitely true?
questions.explanation: B>C≥D=E. Therefore, B>E.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, e.g., R can be represented by 31, 01, etc., and O can be represented by 78, 69, etc. Similarly you have to identify the set for the word ROCK. MATRIX-I 01234 0SRJKL 1KJKSL 2RJSRL 3JRLSK 4JKRLS MATRIX-II 56789 5CDOTP 6TPCDO 7PCDOT 8POCTP 9CPODT
questions.explanation: By verifying using 1st option 31 67 78 10 R C O K By verifying 2nd option 42 69 55 76 R O C C By verifying using 3rd option 01 57 34 03 R O K K By verifying using 4th option 23 86 95 12 R O C K So option (4) is correct.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per order in the dictionary. 1. Penalty 2. Penalize 3. Pendulum 4. Penultimate 5. Pencil 6. Penetrate
questions.explanation: The order is Penalize, Penalty, Pencil, Pendulum, Penetrate, Penultimate, thus 215364.

questions.question: In this question three statements are given that may differ from the commonly known facts. Each statement is followed by two conclusions. You have to give correct answer out of the five options given below: Statement: No pump is dry. All dry are full. All full are null. Conclusion: (I) No pump is null. (II) Some dry being null is a possibility.
options: If neither conclusion I nor II follows
If either conclusion I or II follows
questions.explanation: Since, No + No = No; and No + All = No, but here all dry are full & all full are null = all null are dry or some null are dry. So there is a possibility some null are not dry. So some null can be pump. Hence, conclusion I does not follow. Some dry is null is definitely true from statement II & III. Thus, option II is a possibility. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/28153/image006.jpg)

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the one which can be formed using the letters of the given word. HAILSTORM
questions.explanation: Only the word Hair can be formed by using the given word.

questions.question: If in a certain way, 76 x 85 = 26 and 94 x 36 = 3, then find 87 x 93 = ?

Pointing to a photograph Sahil said, "He is the only son of my mother's only daughter's father's father. How many females are there in the family if there are no other people in the family apart from those mentioned by Mr. Sahil?"
questions.explanation: Sahil's mother's only daughter means Sahil's only sister. Sahil's father's father is his grandfather, so the person in the picture is Sahil's father. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/br-17001.png) Hence there are two females in the family: Sahil's sister and Sahil's mother.

questions.question: If the given interchanges are made in signs and numbers, which one of the four equations would be correct? Given interchange: signs + and - ; numbers '5' and '8'.
Select the related word/letters/number from the given alternatives. EGJP : GJOW :: BDKS : ?
questions.explanation: For this EGJP : GJOW Prime numbers are 2,3,5, 7, ...... E+2=G G+3=J J+5=O P+7=W Similarly follow the above logic BDKS becomes DGPZ.

questions.question: Choose the Venn diagram which best illustrates the three given items using the relationship between these items, match each question with the most suitable diagram. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8411/image004.png) Animals, Men, Plants

questions.question: Select the related word/letters/number from the given alternatives. 49 : 130 :: 78 : ?
A man starts walking towards east at 7 am. After every 20 minutes he takes a left turn and continues walking in the new direction. What direction would he be facing at 11:30 a.m.?
questions.explanation: Time duration between 7 and 11:30 = 4.5 hours = 270 minutes. This means that the man must have taken 13 left turns. 4 left turns bring him to the same direction, so after the 13th left turn he must be facing north.
